tutorial.Introduction:
Welcome!

You can get all the information you need regarding gameplay on this tab. To inquire about gameplay, start by selecting the topic you want on the left side of this menu.

Good luck!
tutorial.Zoning:
Lands, you got a lot of them.

As the government, you need to urbanize and improve this place so that it can become the town you envision. One of the most important things you need to do to achieve this is to sell some of the lands you have, allowing people to build buildings that can benefit the city.
tutorial.Commercial Industry and Farms:
In order for citizens to feed themselves, they need somewhere to work.

Farm
Farms do not require higher education qualifications. They also do not pay well but are a great option for starting cities. Farms employ a significant number of people, do not affect air quality, but exhaust their workers. Once citizens become better educated, they will stop working on farms and move to other jobs if given the opportunity.

Commercial
Commercial workplaces generally require higher education qualifications but pay well and employ a significant number of people. Commercial workplaces do not affect air quality and do not exhaust their workers.

Industrial
Industrial workplaces generally require higher education qualifications but pay well and employ a significant number of people. Industrial workplaces affect air quality and exhaust their workers.
tutorial.Supply:
It is an obligation for the city to supply its citizens with necessary services and utilities.

Capacity and Networks
Services can only supply a fixed amount on a network. The more houses there are on a network, the higher their consumption will be. This is why you should build additional services as demand increases. Keep an eye on the Supply Tab for any networks nearing full capacity.

Water
Water can be obtained by building water pumps. You can then place water pipes from the pump to citizens' houses.

Electricity
Electricity can be generated using solar panels or wind turbines. You can then place cables from the generators to citizens' houses.
tutorial.Education:
Education is crucial for a growing city.

There are three levels of education. Make sure you build all of them when you have the funds. Higher education leads to better opportunities for citizens. It also improves their moral values when they work in government positions.
tutorial.Fire Department and Healthcare:
Fire departments and healthcare facilities are important services that should be built immediately at the start of the game.

Fire Department
Buildings have a small chance of catching fire. If they are left to burn, the effects can be devastating.

Healthcare
Citizens' health can worsen over time as they work. This is why healthcare facilities are important. Otherwise, citizens may fall sick and eventually pass away.
tutorial.Taxes:
Our budget primarily comes from taxes.

Salary tax is collected every day, while land and transportation taxes are collected monthly. To collect land and transportation taxes, a tax office must be built. Adjusting tax rates also has consequences. If tax rates are too high, approval will plummet and citizens may complain or even move out.
tutorial.Budget:
Sometimes our funds are not enough, which is why budget cuts have to be made.

A department's efficiency is linked to its budget. When the budget is high, the department will function well. When the budget is low, the department will perform poorly and may resort to corruption and bribery.
tutorial.Tourism:
Other than taxes, you can also build tourist sites to add more income to the budget.

Building tourist attractions draws visitors from other cities, allowing your city to earn more money. Air quality affects the number of tourists your city can attract.
tutorial.Road Quality:
Due to weather conditions and budget constraints, road quality degrades over time.

A damaged road takes longer to travel across. You can check a road's quality by clicking on it. If its quality falls below 75, replace it by building a new road over it.
